title: helianthemum oelandicum var. aragonense
intro: Helianthemum oelandicum var. aragonense, commonly known as Aragon Rock Rose, is a flowering plant species native to the Iberian Peninsula. It belongs to the Cistaceae family and is used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Spanish herbalism. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers have been used to treat various health issues, such as digestive problems, respiratory infections, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Helianthemum oelandicum var. aragonense can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or topical creams, making it a versatile natural remedy for overall well-being.
